steps.title: Identify what the underlined phrase is doing
steps.content: The researchers fitted mantises with colored filters over each eye. The underlined phrase immediately follows: much like the filters in 3D glasses once worn at movies. The words “much like” signal an explicit comparison. The 3D movie glasses are something most readers already know, so the comparison is there to make the unfamiliar experimental setup easier to picture — a clarifying analogy.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It offers a comparison meant to aid understanding of the praying mantis study.': The phrase uses “much like” to liken the mantis filters to familiar movie glasses, helping readers visualize the setup. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It emphasizes a difference between the research on praying mantis vision and research on other animals' vision.': The phrase compares the filter device to movie glasses, not the mantis research to research on horses or sheep. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It describes an earlier use of a tool the researchers used in the praying mantis study.': The phrase is a simile for explanation, not a history of how 3D glasses were previously used in research. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It identifies a potential problem that the researchers faced while studying the praying mantises.': Nothing in the phrase or the surrounding text suggests a problem with the methodology. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
